audit_items.proof_trace: In the REWRITE_MAP_XLDAPMAP case, `lud->lud_filter` is passed directly as the format-string argument to snprintf (line 384) and `lud->lud_attrs[0]` is dereferenced (line 387) with no NULL guards. `ldap_url_parse` (url.c:892-894) initialises both fields to NULL and only sets them when the corresponding URL component is present; a valid LDAP URL such as `ldap://host/dc=example,dc=com??sub` therefore yields a successfully-parsed descriptor where both fields are NULL. `rewrite_xmap_parse` stores the descriptor without validating these fields, so the crash is deferred to apply-time.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An LDAP rewrite rule is configured with an xldap map whose URL omits the filter and/or attributes component (e.g., `${xldap(ldap://internal/dc=corp,dc=com??sub)}`). Any subsequent external LDAP request that triggers this rewrite rule causes `rewrite_xmap_apply` to reach line 384 (NULL format-string to snprintf) or line 387 (dereference of NULL `lud_attrs`), crashing the OpenLDAP daemon and producing a remotely-triggered denial of service.
